name: Golobar and Valdecebollas Loop
name_original: Parque Natural Montaña Palentina – circular desde Fontibre hasta el refugio El Golobar
description: This difficult 37.0-mile mountain bike route in Parque Natural Montaña Palentina climbs 5527 feet from Fontibre to El Golobar.
region_intro: Mountain biking around Hermandad De Campoo De Suso offers a diverse landscape characterized by mountainous terrain, including significant passes and river valleys. The region features a mix of forested areas and open mountain slopes, providing varied riding conditions. Elevations can be substantial, with routes traversing through high-altitude sections and offering expansive views. The area's geology supports a network of trails suitable for different mountain biking experiences. Best mountain bike trails around Hermandad De Campoo De Suso The most popular mountain bike trail is Transcantábrica - Palombera Pass Loop, a 22.1 miles (35.6 km) trail that takes 3 hours 22 minutes to complete. This route features significant climbs and descents through mountain passes. Another top favourite among local mountain bikers is Golobar and Valdecebollas Loop, a difficult 37.0 miles (59.6 km) path. This trail navigates through high-altitude areas with challenging ascents and panoramic views. Local mountain bikers also love the Palombera Pass – Cruz de Fuentes loop from Reinosa, a 41.3 miles (66.5 km) trail leading through mountain passes and forested sections, often completed in about 6 hours 49 minutes. Mountain biking around Hermandad De Campoo De Suso is defined by mountain passes, river valleys, and extensive forests. The network offers options for easy, moderate, and difficult ability levels. The routes in Hermandad De Campoo De Suso are highly rated by the komoot community with an average score of 4.8 stars from over 60 reviews. More than 700 mountain bikers have used komoot to explore Hermandad De Campoo De Suso's varied terrain.
region_faq:
Q: How many mountain bike trails are available in Hermandad De Campoo De Suso?
A: There are over 75 mountain bike trails available in Hermandad De Campoo De Suso, offering a wide range of experiences for different skill levels.

Q: What are the difficulty levels of mountain bike trails in Hermandad De Campoo De Suso?
A: The trails in Hermandad De Campoo De Suso cater to various skill levels. You'll find 2 easy routes, 46 moderate routes, and 30 difficult routes, ensuring options for beginners to advanced riders.

Q: Are there easy mountain bike trails suitable for beginners in Hermandad De Campoo De Suso?
A: Yes, there are a few easy mountain bike trails perfect for beginners. These routes typically feature less challenging terrain and elevation changes, allowing new riders to enjoy the scenic beauty of the region at a comfortable pace.

Q: Where can I find challenging mountain bike trails in Hermandad De Campoo De Suso?
A: For experienced riders seeking a challenge, trails like the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1000382786/golobar-and-valdecebollas-loop" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Golobar and Valdecebollas Loop&lt;/a&gt; offer significant climbs and descents over 37 miles (59.6 km). Another demanding option is the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/6972899" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Palombera Pass – Cruz de Fuentes loop from Reinosa&lt;/a&gt;, which spans 41.3 miles (66.5 km) through mountain passes and forested sections.

Q: Are there loop mountain bike trails in Hermandad De Campoo De Suso?
A: Many mountain bike trails in Hermandad De Campoo De Suso are designed as loops, allowing you to start and finish at the same point. Examples include the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1000368765/transcantabrica-palombera-pass-loop" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Transcantábrica - Palombera Pass Loop&lt;/a&gt; and the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/24634660" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Cuenca Bucer – Iglesia de Espinilla loop from Reinosa&lt;/a&gt;.

Q: What kind of elevation changes can I expect on mountain bike trails in Hermandad De Campoo De Suso?
A: Hermandad De Campoo De Suso is characterized by mountainous terrain, so you can expect substantial elevation changes. Many routes traverse high-altitude sections and mountain passes, with some difficult trails featuring over 1,600 meters of ascent and descent.

Q: Which mountain bike trails offer the best scenic views in Hermandad De Campoo De Suso?
A: The region is known for its expansive views. Trails that pass through high-altitude areas or near mountain passes often provide stunning panoramas. Consider routes that offer glimpses of highlights like the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/586766" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Piedrasluengas Viewpoint&lt;/a&gt; or the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/4566932" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;El Moral Plateau&lt;/a&gt;.

Q: What natural attractions or landmarks can I see while mountain biking in Hermandad De Campoo De Suso?
A: While mountain biking, you can encounter several natural attractions and landmarks. Notable points of interest include the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/2573626" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Source of the Ebro River&lt;/a&gt;, the historic village of &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/4787483" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Bárcena Mayor&lt;/a&gt;, and the impressive &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/2839687" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Tres Mares Peak&lt;/a&gt;.

Q: When is the best time of year for mountain biking in Hermandad De Campoo De Suso?
A: The best time for mountain biking in Hermandad De Campoo De Suso is typically from late spring to early autumn (May to October). During these months, the weather is generally more stable, and the mountain passes are clear of snow, offering optimal riding conditions and access to all trails.

Q: What do other mountain bikers say about the trails in Hermandad De Campoo De Suso?
A: The mountain bike trails in Hermandad De Campoo De Suso are highly rated by the komoot community, with an average score of 4.8 stars from over 60 reviews. Riders often praise the diverse landscape, challenging climbs, and the stunning views encountered along the routes.

Q: Are there any mountain passes I can cycle through in Hermandad De Campoo De Suso?
A: Yes, the region is known for its mountain passes. You can cycle through significant passes such as &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/3277001" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Palombera Pass&lt;/a&gt; and &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/3268511" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Cruz de Fuentes&lt;/a&gt;, which are often part of longer, more challenging routes.

Q: Can I find mountain bike trails that follow river valleys in Hermandad De Campoo De Suso?
A: Yes, the diverse landscape of Hermandad De Campoo De Suso includes river valleys. Some mountain bike routes incorporate sections that follow these valleys, offering a different riding experience compared to the higher mountain passes.
tips: The town of Fontibre is recognized because right here is the spring that indicates the birthplace of the Ebro River, one of the most important in Spain.
Typical constructions in this area where a large rock was used to serve as a wall and roof and build the rest around it. Some are still used today as a mountain refuge.

This building was part of a project for a Parador de Turismo and ski resort subsidized by the Diputación de Palencia. To this day, the only ones who take advantage of it are the shepherds and their cattle that graze on those mountains.

Chozo Tonino is just off the road, but you'll miss it if you're not looking for it. It's not really a hut, it's a small cave where a Barruelano took refuge

faqs.question: What is the difficulty level of this mountain bike route?
faqs.answer: This circular mountain bike route is classified as difficult. Riders should expect a challenging experience with significant elevation changes, including long and demanding climbs with steep ramps. The terrain can be rugged, featuring a mix of paved roads, gravel tracks, and technical forest trails, especially in higher sections or on descents.

faqs.question: What kind of terrain can I expect on the circular route from Fontibre to El Golobar?
faqs.answer: The route offers diverse terrain, typical of the Montaña Palentina. You'll encounter a mix of paved roads, gravel tracks, and potentially more technical forest trails. Be prepared for sections with loose gravel and continuous inclines, some reaching 10-13% or even a 12% 'wall' near El Golobar. Technical descents are also part of the challenge.

faqs.question: What are the main points of interest along the route?
faqs.answer: Beyond the stunning natural scenery, you'll pass several notable points. The route starts at Fontibre, the picturesque source of the Ebro River. As you ascend, you might encounter the Chozo del Prau del Toru and the Chozo Tonino Cave. The destination, Golobar Refuge, is a significant mountain pass with remnants of an abandoned ski resort project, adding a unique, atmospheric element.

faqs.question: What kind of views can I expect from El Golobar?
faqs.answer: From El Golobar, you can expect breathtaking panoramic views. On clear days, you'll be able to see the Santullán and Valdeolea valleys, Las Tuerces, and even distant peaks like Curavacas, Espigüete, Peña Labra, and the Picos de Europa. The varied landscapes include majestic pine forests, oak and beech woods, and open mountain scenery.

faqs.question: Where can I park to start this mountain bike route?
faqs.answer: The route begins in Fontibre, which is the source of the Ebro River. While specific parking details for this exact route are not provided, Fontibre is a known tourist spot, so you should find suitable parking facilities there to begin your ride.

faqs.question: Is this mountain bike route dog-friendly?
faqs.answer: While the Montaña Palentina Natural Park generally allows dogs, it's crucial to keep them on a leash, especially given the presence of wildlife such as brown bears, Iberian wolves, and golden eagles. Always ensure your dog is well-behaved and that you clean up after them to preserve the natural environment.

faqs.question: What is the best time of year to mountain bike this route?
faqs.answer: The best time to tackle this route is typically during the warmer months, from late spring to early autumn (May to October). During these periods, the weather is generally more stable, and the trails are less likely to be affected by snow or heavy rain, which can make the already challenging terrain even more difficult and potentially dangerous.

faqs.question: Do I need any permits or is there an entrance fee for the Parque Natural Montaña Palentina?
faqs.answer: Generally, there are no entrance fees or specific permits required for mountain biking within the Parque Natural Montaña Palentina. However, it's always advisable to respect local regulations, stay on marked trails, and practice Leave No Trace principles to protect the park's rich biodiversity and natural beauty.

faqs.question: Does this route intersect with any other well-known trails?
faqs.answer: Yes, this route overlaps with sections of several other trails. You'll find it intersects with parts of the [CIMA PA01] Golobar * Salcedillo, the Asturian Way, Sendero Histórico: Palencia, and [CIMA PA01] Golobar * Barruelo de Santullán. Smaller sections also coincide with Sendero Histórico: Cantabria, Camino natural de Ebro - Cantabria, Transcantábrica, Camino de las Asturias, Ruta del Puente de Rojadillo, and Puente de Rojadillo.

faqs.question: What wildlife might I encounter in the Montaña Palentina Natural Park?
faqs.answer: The Montaña Palentina Natural Park is renowned for its ecological richness. While mountain biking, you might have the chance to spot diverse wildlife, including species like the brown bear, Iberian wolf, and golden eagle. Keep an eye out for other local fauna and flora that thrive in this protected environment.

faqs.question: Are there any facilities like cafes or accommodation near the starting point in Fontibre?
faqs.answer: Fontibre is a popular starting point for exploring the region, and as such, you can expect to find some basic facilities. While specific cafes or accommodation directly at the trailhead are not detailed, the nearby villages and towns in the Montaña Palentina area offer various options for refreshments, meals, and overnight stays.
